skill.name: research-storage
skill.description: Defines project-local research cache conventions, file templates, path normalization, and lookup rules for dokhak research agents.
skill.summary: Store and reuse dokhak research files with consistent paths, templates, and lookup rules.
skill.author: BitYoungjae

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many shell backtick, weak crypto, reconnaissance, and obfuscation patterns. Manual review found these are Markdown fences, inline file names, placeholders, and documentation examples, not executable code. The only confirmed risk is intended project-local filesystem access through Read, Write, and Glob for `.research/` cache files.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static external command findings point to Markdown code fences, inline path examples, and Read, Glob, or Write examples. The skill has no shell tool, Ruby code, or command execution instructions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited text is inside Markdown examples and references allowed skill tools. No executable shell, Ruby backtick execution, or command interpreter invocation is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Detections
low_findings.description: The cited locations do not contain cryptographic algorithms or hash calls. They contain YAML metadata, Markdown templates, placeholder text, or headings related to research files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no MD5, SHA1, crypto API, or password storage logic at the cited lines. The detections appear caused by Markdown file names or unrelated text.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Obfuscation and Reconnaissance Signals
low_findings.description: The reported obfuscation and system reconnaissance locations are front matter, Markdown tables, and template placeholders. They do not contain JavaScript obfuscation or host reconnaissance commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The bracket-heavy syntax is normal YAML, Markdown, and template notation. No code executes, collects host details, or hides behavior.

low_findings.title: Intended Project-Local Filesystem Writes
low_findings.description: The skill authorizes Read, Write, and Glob and instructs agents to create or update `.research/` files. This is legitimate for the skill, but it can modify project-local cache files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The declared tools and documented paths clearly allow local file reads, writes, and glob searches. The scope is constrained to project-relative `.research/` paths and shows no malicious intent.

content.value_statement: Research agents need stable storage rules to avoid duplicate work and inconsistent paths. This skill defines cache directories, file templates, lookup tiers, and response formats.
actual_capabilities: Provides templates for summary, source registry, and section research files.
Describes chapter, section, and title normalization rules.
Defines multi-tier Glob lookup for legacy directory names.
Specifies XML status formats for research and directory resolution.
limitations: It does not perform web research or collect sources by itself.
It does not verify the factual accuracy of stored research.
It assumes agents have Read, Write, and Glob permissions.
It stores files locally and does not provide sync or database features.

use_cases.title: Standardize Research Output
use_cases.description: Use consistent folder names and Markdown templates for research generated by multiple agents.

use_cases.title: Reuse Existing Section Research
use_cases.description: Resolve existing research files before starting new collection work for a chapter section.

use_cases.title: Maintain Source Registries
use_cases.description: Track primary sources, secondary sources, rejected sources, and coverage status in predictable files.

prompt_templates.title: Create Init Research Files
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the research-storage conventions to create initial research summary and source registry files for my project topic.

prompt_templates.title: Resolve a Section Path
prompt_templates.scenario: Find or create a cache location
prompt_templates.prompt: Resolve the research directory for chapter 1, section 2, titled Core Concepts, using the multi-tier lookup rules.

prompt_templates.title: Save Section Research
prompt_templates.prompt: Format my collected notes and sources as a section research file and matching source registry using this skill.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Coverage Across Legacy Paths
prompt_templates.prompt: Check whether legacy directory naming could hide existing research, then summarize coverage and missing subtopics.

output_examples.input: Resolve storage for chapter 3, section 1, Getting Started.
output_examples.output: The canonical directory is `.research/sections/03-1-getting-started/`. If no matching legacy file exists, use that path for new research.

output_examples.input: Save source notes for an initial research pass.
output_examples.output: The result includes a research summary, a source registry, reliability labels, and a short confirmation with the saved cache path.

output_examples.input: Review whether a section has complete research coverage.
output_examples.output: The response identifies complete, partial, and missing subtopics, then points to the resolved research file for follow-up work.

best_practices: Normalize chapter, section, and title values before writing new research files.
Use the multi-tier lookup before starting fresh research for a section.
Keep source reliability and rejected-source notes with each research file.
anti_patterns: Do not write section research without checking for existing legacy paths first.
Do not store unrelated project files inside the `.research/` cache directory.
Do not treat cached research as verified unless sources were reviewed recently.

faq.question: Does this skill perform research automatically?
faq.answer: No. It defines how other agents should store, find, and format research results.

faq.question: Which tools does it require?
faq.answer: The skill declares Read, Write, and Glob for project-local research files.

faq.question: Where are files stored?
faq.answer: Files are stored under `.research/init/` or `.research/sections/` within the project.

faq.question: Can it handle older directory names?
faq.answer: Yes. It defines lookup tiers for padded chapters, unpadded chapters, slug changes, and keyword matches.

faq.question: Is it safe for read-only consumers?
faq.answer: Yes, if consumers only use Read and Glob. Writing agents can modify `.research/` cache files.

faq.question: Does it replace a database?
faq.answer: No. It is a file-based convention for local Markdown research cache files.
